title: Design real-time fraud detection (Stripe Radar) + DSA review
focus: Velocity / graph / behavioral features; delayed labels; cost-sensitive thresholds.

items.label: Design real-time fraud detection (Stripe/PayPal)
interviewQuestions: Walk me through designing Stripe Radar — real-time fraud detection.
How do you balance false positives (blocked good users) vs false negatives (fraud)?

items.label: Feature engineering: velocity, graph, behavioral
interviewQuestions: What kinds of features (velocity, graph, behavioral) actually catch fraud?
How do you serve graph-based fraud features in real-time without exploding compute?

items.label: Delayed labels & feedback loops
interviewQuestions: Fraud labels arrive weeks late (chargebacks). How do you train and monitor under delayed labels?
How would you use early proxy signals (e.g., risk score, manual review) when chargebacks are slow?

items.label: Clarify requirements, users, constraints, and business metric for: Design real-time fraud detection (Stripe Radar)
interviewQuestions: What are the first five clarifying questions before proposing any model?
What is the business metric, what is the ML proxy metric, and where can they diverge?
What scale, latency, freshness, privacy, and reliability constraints change the architecture?

items.label: Defend data, labels, baseline, model choice, feature freshness, and serving path for: Design real-time fraud detection (Stripe Radar)
interviewQuestions: What baseline would you launch before a complex model, and how would you know it is insufficient?
Where do labels come from, what bias do they contain, and how delayed are they?
Which features must be computed online vs offline, and what happens when the feature store is slow?

items.label: Cover monitoring, rollback, cost, security, failure modes, and launch plan for: Design real-time fraud detection (Stripe Radar)
interviewQuestions: What online metrics, guardrails, and cohort slices decide whether this system is safe to ramp?
What is the rollback trigger, fallback experience, and incident owner if quality or latency regresses?
What are the top security, privacy, abuse, or cost risks, and what controls would you ship in v1?

references.label: Chip Huyen — Machine Learning Systems Design
Google Cloud Professional ML Engineer exam guide
Google ML system design mock interview — YouTube recommendations
